# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Http\Session;

use Illuminate\Contracts\Session\Session as SessionContract;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie;

class CookieBasedPreferencesHandler implements \SessionHandlerInterface
{
    private $lifetime = 86400 * 7; // A week in seconds
    
    public function open($savePath, $sessionName) {
        return true;
    }
    
    public function close() {
        return true;
    }
    
    public function read($id) {
        $preferences = unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']);
        
        if (is_array($preferences)) {
            // Restore the preferences object with the data from the cookie
            $this->restorePreferencesFromArray($preferences);
            
            return true;
        }
        
        return false;
    }
    
    public function write($id, $data) {
        $preferences = $this->getPreferencesAsArray();

        // Set the data to be serialized in the cookie
        setcookie('user_prefs', serialize($data), time() + $this->lifetime);
        
        return true;
    }
    
    public function destroy($id) {
        // Delete the user preferences cookie by setting its expiration date in the past
        setcookie('user_prefs', '', time() - 3600);
        
        return true;
    }
    
    public function gc($maxlifetime) {
        // Cookies are automatically deleted when they expire, so no garbage collection needed.
        return true;
    }
    
    private function getPreferencesAsArray() {
        // Implement this based on your application structure to retrieve the current preferences object as an array
        
        // For example:
        // return [
        //   'theme' => 'dark',
        //   'language' => 'en'
        // ];
    }
    
    private function restorePreferencesFromArray($preferences) {
        // Implement this based on your application structure to restore the preferences object from an array
        
        // For example:
        // $this->setTheme($preferences['theme']);
        // $this->setLanguage($preferences['language']);
    }
}
